sections-labeled.description: Bold headers matching input talking points

input-01.note: Headers: 'The Willpower Myth', 'What Metabolic Adaptation Actually Is', 'Why Your Body Fights Weight Loss', 'Three Evidence-Based Strategies That Work With Your Metabolism', 'Why Consistency Matters More Than Perfection' — all match input talking points

input-02.note: Headers match all 5 talking points: microplastics origins, blood-brain barrier study, what we know vs don't, misleading media coverage, what you can do

input-03.note: Headers: 'Why People Don't Read', 'Simple Habits That Actually Work', 'What to Read' — match all 3 input talking points

input-04.note: Headers match all 5 talking points: Golden Visa programme, Lisbon property prices, displacement of renters, 2023 policy reversal, lessons for Europe

input-05.note: Headers for each life lesson section: 'Most Opinions Do Not Matter', 'Your Energy Is More Valuable Than Your Time', etc. — expands vague 'life lessons' input into specific sections

direction-cues.description: [square bracket] direction notes at least twice

input-01.note: Direction cues: [cut to study graphic or text on screen], [beat — let the audience absorb], [on screen: graphic showing diet break cycle], [look directly at camera] — 4 cues

input-02.note: Direction cues: [cut to b-roll], [beat], [on screen: two-column comparison], [look at camera], [cut to simple graphic] — 5 cues

input-03.note: Direction cues: [look at camera, casual], [on screen: '15 min/day = ~20 books/year'], [beat] — 3 cues

input-04.note: Direction cues: [cut to archival], [on screen: graph], [beat], [cut to news footage], [on screen: text] — 5 cues

input-05.note: Direction cues: [beat], [look at camera], [on screen: 'Everyone is making it up.'], [beat], [look at camera], [beat] — 6 cues

cta-included.description: Ends with specific CTA in outro

input-01.note: CTA: 'share this video with them' — matches requested CTA 'Share this with someone who needs to hear it'

input-02.note: CTA: 'drop them in the comments. I will read everything and use them to shape the next video' — matches requested CTA about comment questions for follow-up

input-03.note: CTA: 'let me know what you are reading right now in the comments' — specific comment prompt (no explicit CTA was requested, skill generated an appropriate one)

input-04.note: CTA: 'subscribe. I cover these stories because the patterns repeat across borders' — matches requested CTA 'Subscribe for more European housing stories'

input-05.note: CTA: 'let me know in the comments which one you needed to hear today' — specific comment prompt (no explicit CTA was requested, skill generated an appropriate one)

summary.notes: All 5 outputs demonstrate consistent structure: word count header, labeled hook, bold section headers matching talking points, multiple direction cues in square brackets, and specific CTAs in the outro. Vague inputs (03, 05) were handled well — the skill expanded minimal talking points into full sections and generated appropriate CTAs when none were specified.
